content:
- Commit completed successfully as `babdee8`; the documented post-commit hook also rebuilt/published the mdBook and pushed docs repo commit `bdbb8dd5`.
- Pushing the task commit now; if the worktree is clean afterward, this task is fully closed.

- Read the previous Choose-Task progress log, then inspected `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`.
- Verified the active backlog contains six failing tasks, no active bug tasks, no active meta-task, and no `<blocked_by>` tags on the active candidates I inspected.
- Compared priorities and current repo state. The two switchover tasks are explicitly `<priority>low</priority>`, so they lose to untagged failing work. Among the untagged tasks, the phantom config-versioning cleanup is the most urgent because the false `config_version` model is still spread across live parser code, tests, docker runtime configs, and multiple docs/draft/tmp artifacts.
- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with `.ralph/tasks/story-secure-explicit-node-config/07-task-remove-phantom-config-versioning-and-restore-single-config-contract.md`.
- Next time: execute that task, remove the fake config versioning model end-to-end, and verify make check/test/lint.

- Read previous progress log entry and current task pointer.
- Identified active task file: .ralph/tasks/story-secure-explicit-node-config/07-task-remove-phantom-config-versioning-and-restore-single-config-contract.md.
- Reading task file now to determine whether it needs an initial plan, skeptical verification, or direct execution.

- Read the active task file for story-secure-explicit-node-config task 07.
- Found that a detailed plan was already present, but the required workflow marker was missing.
- Appended `TO BE VERIFIED` to the task file so the next engineer/pass must perform a skeptical plan review before any execution.
- Stopping immediately after the planning phase, per task instructions.

- Read the active task file for story-secure-explicit-node-config task 07 and confirmed it was in the TO BE VERIFIED phase.
- Ran a repo-wide scan for config_version/runtime-config v1-v2 references to review the plan skeptically against actual tracked files.
- Found plan gaps: tracked built-book output under docs/book/searchindex.json and a wider set of tracked docs/tmp how-to/tutorial/explanation prompt artifacts also repeat the false runtime-config versioning story.
- Also confirmed src/debug_api/worker.rs config_version hits appear unrelated to runtime-config schema gating and should be treated as intentional exclusions unless later evidence disproves that.
- Updated the task plan to include those additional tracked artifacts, replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE, and stopped before implementation so the next pass can execute the approved plan directly.

- Read progress log and current task pointer at startup as required.
- Confirmed active task is story-secure-explicit-node-config task 07 and prior pass already advanced its plan to NOW EXECUTE.
- Reading the task file now so I can execute the approved plan directly without additional exploratory drift.

- Opened the active task file and confirmed it is in NOW EXECUTE.
- Starting execution with the approved grep gate to enumerate all remaining runtime-config versioning references and distinguish real cleanup targets from intentional exclusions.
- Will patch the listed config code/tests/docs/config files next and then run the full required make targets.

- Began the implementation edit pass.
- Applied the broad v2-input type rename across config schema/parser/defaults/mod so the code can describe one direct runtime-config input shape instead of a schema epoch.
- Applied an initial string cleanup pass removing config_version=v2 phrasing from validation/docs before doing the structural parser patch.

- Core parser/config changes are in place: load_runtime_config now deserializes the single RuntimeConfigInput directly and no longer gates on config_version.
- Broad literal scrub removed config_version lines from runtime TOMLs/tests/docs, but generated and draft doc artifacts still need one more cleanup/regeneration pass.
- A focused cargo test compile is running to surface Rust breakage before the full make targets.

- make check passed successfully after the single-shape runtime-config rewrite.
- Remaining work is the heavier gates (`make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`) plus docs artifact cleanup/regeneration and task-file checklist updates.
- Current known intentional exclusions for grep are the real debug snapshot version counters in src/debug_api/worker.rs and prompt mirrors that still refer to those counters rather than runtime-config schema gating.

- make test passed: 396 tests run, 396 passed, 9 skipped by profile.
- Proceeding to make test-long, then make lint, then docs/book refresh and final task metadata updates.

- make test-long passed, including 9 ultra-long HA scenarios and both Docker smoke checks.
- Running make lint now.
- After lint: rebuild docs/book, verify the grep gate with documented exclusions, then update the task file and finish the task-switch/commit/push sequence.

- make lint failed because the legacy Partial* schema types in src/config/schema.rs are now dead code after removing the old config_version envelope/probing path.
- Fixing this by removing the unused partial schema block entirely instead of adding lint allowances.

- Marked the task file completed with passes=true, checklist ticks, execution notes, intentional exclusions, and verification evidence.
- Running Ralph task_switch now, then I will stage all changes, commit with the required task-finished message, and push.
